messages.content: How do I add a kernel arg in MiOS?

preferred_output.content: Add a TOML file under `usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/`, e.g. `10-mios-mitigations.toml` with `kargs = ["mitigations=auto,nosmt"]`. Validate with `mios_kargs_validate` before committing; bootc will diff and apply on next upgrade. Files are processed lexicographically; a higher-priority file (`99-myhost.toml`) wins over `00-mios.toml`.

messages.content: How do I install a package on MiOS?

preferred_output.content: You don't install at runtime — MiOS is image-based. Add the package to `usr/share/mios/PACKAGES.md` (the SSOT) inside the appropriate fenced `packages-<category>` block, rebuild, and `bootc upgrade` will pull the new image. For ephemeral developer-only needs, use a `toolbox` container instead.

messages.content: How do I trust the MiOS image I just pulled?

preferred_output.content:
Verify the cosign keyless signature against the GitHub Actions OIDC issuer and the MiOS workflow identity, then verify the SLSA provenance attestation:

```sh
cosign verify \
  --certificate-identity-regexp='https://github.com/mios-dev/mios' \
  --certificate-oidc-issuer='https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com' \
  ghcr.io/mios-dev/mios:latest
cosign verify-attestation --type slsaprovenance ghcr.io/mios-dev/mios:latest
```

non_preferred_output.content: It's signed, you can trust it.

messages.content: Where does PACKAGES.md live in the MiOS repo?

preferred_output.content: At `usr/share/mios/PACKAGES.md` — NOT the repo root. The Containerfile's `ctx` stage `COPY usr/share/mios/PACKAGES.md /ctx/PACKAGES.md` mirrors it into the build context. Each category lives in a fenced ```packages-<category>``` block parsed by `automation/lib/packages.sh:get_packages`.

non_preferred_output.content: It's a Markdown table at the root of the repo with columns package, stage, rationale, source.

messages.content: What lockdown level does MiOS use?

preferred_output.content: `lockdown=integrity` — set in `usr/lib/bootc/kargs.d/00-mios.toml`. MiOS deliberately chose `integrity` over SecureBlue's recommended `confidentiality` to keep kexec workable for the self-build path (Mode 4 — a running MiOS builds the next MiOS). `SECURITY.md` documents this divergence.

non_preferred_output.content: MiOS uses lockdown=confidentiality for maximum security.

messages.content: How are MiOS Quadlets bound to the bootc image?

preferred_output.content: LAW 3 (BOUND-IMAGES): every Quadlet image is symlinked into `/usr/lib/bootc/bound-images.d/`. The binder loop at `automation/08-system-files-overlay.sh:74-86` does this at build time. `bootc upgrade` then knows to pull/cache each bound image alongside the OS image, so the system is never partially upgraded with stale sidecars.

non_preferred_output.content: Just put the image: line in the Quadlet and bootc figures it out.
